_format.note: Quiet events — non-combat encounters that appear on the corridor as a QUIET node. Each event has a title (page tag), prose (1-3 paragraphs setting the scene), and 2-3 choices, each with a label, voice (the protagonist's reason or hesitation), an outcome (paragraph shown after picking), and effects (an array of effect objects applied to run state). Effects: { heal: N }, { damage: N }, { addAttachment: 'id' or 'random' }, { gainComposureMax: N }.

the_lounge.title: // quiet · the staff lounge · after hours
prose: The door was open. The light was on. A cup of coffee was still warm on the counter.
There was a chart on the table. The page was open to my designation. The pen was capped beside it.

choices.label: Take the coffee.
choices.voice: It is still warm. Someone has left it for me. ~~Someone has left.~~
choices.outcome: It was bitter. I felt steadier for a few minutes. The cup was warm in my hands long after it should not have been.

choices.label: Take the pen.
choices.voice: If they will write me here, I want one of theirs.
choices.outcome: I put it in the inside pocket of the gown. I felt it against my chest for the rest of the descent.

choices.label: Leave the room without touching anything.
choices.voice: Whoever left this was waiting for me to take it.
choices.outcome: I closed the door behind me. The hallway smelled of soap. ~~Less of me had been spent here.~~

the_window.title: // quiet · the east hallway · the window
prose: The window was at the end of the hallway. The light through it was the same as it had been when I came in.
There was no garden beyond it. ~~There was, once.~~ I could see my reflection in the glass instead.

choices.label: Sit by the window for a long time.
choices.voice: I would like to feel the light without anything in it.
choices.outcome: It was hard to leave. When I did, I felt warmer than I had since admission. !!Less of me had been left in the rooms.!!

choices.label: Knock on the glass.
choices.outcome: My reflection knocked back. We watched each other for a while. ~~The reflection went first.~~ I went next.

the_old_file.title: // quiet · the records corridor · a cabinet left open
prose: A cabinet drawer had been left open. The folders were dated from before I had been born.
One of them had my name on it. ~~My new name.~~ My old one.

choices.label: Open the file with my name.
choices.voice: I would like to know what I forgot.
choices.outcome: There was a name in it that I have not heard since I was a child. The name was crossed out. ~~The new name was the same.~~

choices.label: Close the drawer.
choices.voice: I do not need to know.
choices.outcome: I closed it. I felt steadier afterward. The corridor was longer than it had been before. ~~I did not look back.~~

the_orderly.title: // quiet · the corridor · an orderly waiting
prose: The orderly was leaning against the wall with her arms folded. She had been at the door of my room earlier.
She nodded when I came around the corner. ~~She said she was glad to see me.~~ She said the room would be ready when I was.

choices.label: Walk past her without speaking.
choices.voice: I am tired of the staff knowing my name before I do.
choices.outcome: She let me. She did not look up. The corridor smelled of the floor wax it had smelled of the day I was admitted.

choices.label: Ask her about the room she said would be ready.
choices.voice: I do not know what room she means. ~~I do.~~
choices.outcome: She smiled, briefly, the way the staff do when a patient has asked a question for the first time. She said the room would still be there when I got down to it.

the_bench.title: // quiet · the south corridor · the bench
prose: There was a bench in the south corridor that had not been there the last time I passed. It was made of the same wood as the chairs in the rooms.
Sitting on it was someone I did not know. ~~Who I knew.~~ They did not look up.

choices.label: Sit beside them. Do not speak.
choices.voice: We have both been in the rooms. We do not need to be introduced.
choices.outcome: We sat for a while. They did not move. When I stood to leave, they tapped the bench once. ~~In thanks.~~ I tapped it once. I left.

choices.label: Keep walking.
choices.voice: I do not want to know who is on the bench.
choices.outcome: I passed without looking. ~~The tap behind me was the bench, settling.~~
